# Jira Fronting Showcase

Canonical governed Jira fronting showcase package.

This package exposes ANIP capabilities for bounded Jira reads, preview-only issue preparation, approval-gated Jira changes, audit evidence, and explicit denial of raw Jira/JQL/tool access.

Project: a7c941cf-b552-4d5b-a361-e649591ff5f8
Package: jira-fronting-showcase@0.2.3
Product revision: r1
Developer revision: r1
Contract signature: 2caf110cd3fd6a3763c09454db3ca5d6c0c6a84d03ede134b8a2cfcc0197f29e

## Generate

From a downloaded package bundle:

```bash
go run ./cmd/anip-generate --package-bundle jira-fronting-showcase-0.2.3.anip-package.json --target python --dependency-source registry --output ./generated/jira-fronting-showcase --force
```

From a trusted registry package:

```bash
go run ./cmd/anip-generate --registry-url <registry-url> --package-id jira-fronting-showcase --package-version 0.2.3 --target python --dependency-source registry --output ./generated/jira-fronting-showcase --force
```

## Verify

```bash
go run ./cmd/anip-verify --definition anip-service-definition.json
go run ./cmd/anip-verify --registry-url <registry-url> --package-id jira-fronting-showcase --package-version 0.2.3
```

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read-only context retrieval can proceed within bounded scope, while write-adjacent actions either stop at preview or require explicit approval before any Jira mutation is executed. Higher-impact changes are approval-gated and auditable.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product exposes governed Jira capabilities instead of raw Jira operations. Reads return bounded actor-visible context, write-adjacent actions produce previews or requests rather than direct execution, missing required inputs trigger clarification, and unsafe raw access requests are explicitly denied.

coordination.description: Preview-only preparation and bounded reads stay in `jira-fronting`; approval-gated change requests and control decisions route to `jira-governance` when policy or mutation risk requires it.
Governance outcomes return approval-stop, denial, or audit-linked response context that the fronting experience can present to the actor.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Users can retrieve bounded Jira backlog or issue context through governed capabilities without being exposed to raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or unbounded JQL-style access.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Scenario review shows backlog search and issue context flows return actor-visible bounded results, while requests for raw Jira or raw query/tool access are explicitly denied or redirected to governed capabilities.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Issue, subtask, bug, story, customer comment, workflow, sprint, assignee, and issue-link flows produce previews or approval requests before any Jira mutation is allowed.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative flow review shows write-adjacent actions stop at preview or approval request states and do not execute direct Jira changes without the required approval boundary.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: When required inputs are missing, ambiguous, or unresolved, the product asks for clarification instead of guessing or silently failing.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Review scenarios across search, issue lookup, assignee resolution, and change-request flows show explicit clarification prompts for missing or ambiguous project, issue, user, sprint, status, or relationship inputs.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Approval-required Jira change requests provide enough preview detail and business reason context for a change approver to review the intended action before execution.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM review of workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue link request examples shows the target change, affected Jira items, and stated reason are visible at approval time.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: The showcase produces reviewable audit evidence for governed reads, denials, previews, approvals, and approved change execution paths.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Audit review can inspect traceable records for representative read, denial, preview, approval-stop, and approved-action scenarios without reconstructing the flow from raw backend logs alone.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Release-note preparation produces a bounded draft from Jira issue and release context without publishing externally or mutating Jira.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Scenario review shows release-note preparation returns a draft artifact based on bounded Jira inputs and does not trigger Jira updates or external dispatch.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can search backlog context, inspect issue context, prepare incident bugs, prepare subtasks, and prepare customer escalation comments within governed input rules and clarification flows.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Operational user who inspects bounded Jira issue and backlog context and prepares issue or comment drafts without direct raw Jira tool access.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Can initiate preview-oriented and approval-gated requests but should stop at explicit approval boundaries for governed mutations.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see bounded Jira project, issue, comment, changelog, and backlog context exposed through governed capabilities, plus previews and audit-relevant request context. Should not see raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or unbounded JQL/tool access.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, and issue links through governed preview and request flows with stated business reasons.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Planning-oriented user who prepares and requests governed changes to issue workflow, sprint placement, assignee, and issue relationships.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Can assemble and submit approval-required change requests, but execution authority is separate unless you confirm this actor is also an approver.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see actor-visible workflow, sprint, assignee-resolution, and issue-link preview context needed to understand proposed changes, but not raw backend operations or unrestricted Jira administration surfaces.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can prepare release notes drafts from governed Jira release and issue queries and tailor drafts for internal, customer, or executive audiences where allowed.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Release-focused user who prepares bounded release notes from Jira issue context without publishing or mutating Jira.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Can prepare release-note drafts directly; approval or publication authority is not specified in the brief and should be clarified if needed.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see bounded release and issue context sufficient to draft release notes for an intended audience, without raw export or unrestricted issue extraction.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue-link requests and decide whether they should proceed under governance.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Reviewing actor who evaluates governed Jira change requests before any approval-required mutation proceeds.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Acts as the explicit approval boundary for approval-required Jira changes, but exact authority by action type is not specified in the source and should be confirmed.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect the proposed change, target issue or sprint context, stated reason, and supporting audit evidence needed to approve or deny governed Jira mutations.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review evidence of bounded reads, prepared write-adjacent actions, approval stops, and explicit denials of raw Jira/JQL/tool access.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Oversight actor who reviews audit evidence for governed Jira access, prepared changes, approvals, and denials.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Typically not the operational approver unless your process combines audit and approval responsibilities; this should be clarified.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect audit evidence showing what governed capability was used, what was requested or denied, and where approval boundaries were enforced. Should not gain raw Jira tool access through the audit role.

linked_product_design_items.detail: Can see bounded Jira project, issue, comment, changelog, and backlog context exposed through governed capabilities, plus previews and audit-relevant request context. Should not see raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or unbounded JQL/tool access.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Delivery Coordinator
linked_product_design_items.detail: Planning-oriented user who prepares and requests governed changes to issue workflow, sprint placement, assignee, and issue relationships.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Delivery Coordinator visibility
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can see actor-visible workflow, sprint, assignee-resolution, and issue-link preview context needed to understand proposed changes, but not raw backend operations or unrestricted Jira administration surfaces.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Release Preparer
linked_product_design_items.detail: Release-focused user who prepares bounded release notes from Jira issue context without publishing or mutating Jira.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Release Preparer visibility
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can see bounded release and issue context sufficient to draft release notes for an intended audience, without raw export or unrestricted issue extraction.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Change Approver
linked_product_design_items.detail: Reviewing actor who evaluates governed Jira change requests before any approval-required mutation proceeds.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Change Approver visibility
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can inspect the proposed change, target issue or sprint context, stated reason, and supporting audit evidence needed to approve or deny governed Jira mutations.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Audit Reviewer
linked_product_design_items.detail: Oversight actor who reviews audit evidence for governed Jira access, prepared changes, approvals, and denials.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Audit Reviewer visibility
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can inspect audit evidence showing what governed capability was used, what was requested or denied, and where approval boundaries were enforced. Should not gain raw Jira tool access through the audit role.

sections.label: Authority & Approval
sections.description: How restrictions, denials, approvals, and clarification posture become formal service behavior.

linked_product_design_items.label: Approval Posture Summary
linked_product_design_items.detail: Read-only context retrieval can proceed within bounded scope, while write-adjacent actions either stop at preview or require explicit approval before any Jira mutation is executed. Higher-impact changes are approval-gated and auditable.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Triage Operator approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can initiate preview-oriented and approval-gated requests but should stop at explicit approval boundaries for governed mutations.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Delivery Coordinator approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can assemble and submit approval-required change requests, but execution authority is separate unless you confirm this actor is also an approver.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Release Preparer approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can prepare release-note drafts directly; approval or publication authority is not specified in the brief and should be clarified if needed.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Change Approver approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Acts as the explicit approval boundary for approval-required Jira changes, but exact authority by action type is not specified in the source and should be confirmed.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Audit Reviewer approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Typically not the operational approver unless your process combines audit and approval responsibilities; this should be clarified.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Triage Operator · Bounded Jira Context
linked_product_design_items.detail: Bounded · Bounded Result · Allow bounded Jira backlog and issue context needed for triage without exposing raw JQL, raw tool access, or unbounded export.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Triage Operator · Issue and Comment Preparation
linked_product_design_items.detail: Bounded · Bounded Result · Allow preparation previews for incident bugs, subtasks, and customer escalation comments without creating issues or posting comments directly.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Delivery Coordinator · Governed Change Requests
linked_product_design_items.detail: Approval Required · Approval Stop · Allow workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue-link requests to be prepared with preview and audit context, then stop for explicit approval before execution.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Release Preparer · Release Note Preparation
linked_product_design_items.detail: Bounded · Bounded Result · Allow preparation of release-note drafts from bounded Jira issue and release context without publishing or mutating Jira.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Change Approver · Governed Change Requests
linked_product_design_items.detail: Approval Required · Approval Stop · Receive governed change-request packages with preview and rationale, and act as the explicit approval boundary before any Jira mutation proceeds.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Audit Reviewer · Governance and Audit Controls
linked_product_design_items.detail: Allowed · Direct Result · Allow access to governed audit evidence and review artifacts for Jira fronting decisions, approvals, and denials.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Triage Operator · Governance and Audit Controls
linked_product_design_items.detail: Denied · Deny Request · Deny requests for raw Jira REST access, MCP access, raw JQL execution, or direct tool use outside the governed capability surface.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Delivery Coordinator · Governance and Audit Controls
linked_product_design_items.detail: Denied · Deny Request · Deny direct raw Jira, MCP, and raw JQL access even when coordinating change requests; use governed request surfaces only.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Release Preparer · Governance and Audit Controls
linked_product_design_items.detail: Denied · Deny Request · Deny raw Jira/JQL/tool access for release-note work; release-note preparation must use bounded governed capabilities only.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Triage Operator · Bounded Jira Context
linked_product_design_items.detail: Restricted · Clarification Required · When required Jira identifiers or scope inputs are missing or ambiguous, stop and ask for clarification instead of guessing project, issue, status, or query scope.

backend_bindings.status_detail: Template-suggested native API implementation seam. Review before locking.

- Describe the required coordination edges between services: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.

- Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.

manifest.readme:
# Jira Fronting Showcase

Canonical governed Jira fronting showcase package.

This package exposes ANIP capabilities for bounded Jira reads, preview-only issue preparation, approval-gated Jira changes, audit evidence, and explicit denial of raw Jira/JQL/tool access.

Project: a7c941cf-b552-4d5b-a361-e649591ff5f8
Package: jira-fronting-showcase@0.2.3
Product revision: r1
Developer revision: r1
Contract signature: 2caf110cd3fd6a3763c09454db3ca5d6c0c6a84d03ede134b8a2cfcc0197f29e

## Generate

From a downloaded package bundle:

```bash
go run ./cmd/anip-generate --package-bundle jira-fronting-showcase-0.2.3.anip-package.json --target python --dependency-source registry --output ./generated/jira-fronting-showcase --force
```

From a trusted registry package:

```bash
go run ./cmd/anip-generate --registry-url <registry-url> --package-id jira-fronting-showcase --package-version 0.2.3 --target python --dependency-source registry --output ./generated/jira-fronting-showcase --force
```

## Verify

```bash
go run ./cmd/anip-verify --definition anip-service-definition.json
go run ./cmd/anip-verify --registry-url <registry-url> --package-id jira-fronting-showcase --package-version 0.2.3
```

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read-only context retrieval can proceed within bounded scope, while write-adjacent actions either stop at preview or require explicit approval before any Jira mutation is executed. Higher-impact changes are approval-gated and auditable.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product exposes governed Jira capabilities instead of raw Jira operations. Reads return bounded actor-visible context, write-adjacent actions produce previews or requests rather than direct execution, missing required inputs trigger clarification, and unsafe raw access requests are explicitly denied.

project.name: Jira Fronting Showcase 0.2.3
project.summary: Canonical Jira fronting showcase package for governed Jira reads, preview-only preparation, approval-gated changes, audit evidence, and safe denial of raw tool access.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not expose raw Jira REST, MCP, or raw tool access to agents or end users.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The brief repeatedly states that the agent must see governed Jira capabilities rather than native operations, and that raw tool access should be safely denied.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not support raw JQL entry or unbounded Jira issue export.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The backlog and issue-context capabilities are defined as bounded, actor-visible reads without raw JQL exposure or unbounded export.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not create, transition, assign, link, comment on, or move Jira issues automatically without the intended preview or approval stop.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: Write-adjacent actions are described as prepare-only or approval-required, with explicit stops before execution.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not guess missing required inputs for Jira actions; require clarification instead.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The governance posture explicitly says missing required inputs should trigger clarification rather than guessing.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not publish or distribute release notes automatically; only prepare bounded drafts.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: Release-note capability scope is limited to preparation from bounded Jira issues and explicitly excludes publishing or mutation.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not return Jira data beyond bounded actor-visible context.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: Reads are defined as bounded and actor-visible, and the project centers on governed Jira context rather than broad data retrieval.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not treat audit evidence as optional or secondary to convenience automation.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The project summary explicitly includes audit evidence as part of the canonical governed showcase.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Users can retrieve bounded Jira backlog or issue context through governed capabilities without being exposed to raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or unbounded JQL-style access.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Scenario review shows backlog search and issue context flows return actor-visible bounded results, while requests for raw Jira or raw query/tool access are explicitly denied or redirected to governed capabilities.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Issue, subtask, bug, story, customer comment, workflow, sprint, assignee, and issue-link flows produce previews or approval requests before any Jira mutation is allowed.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative flow review shows write-adjacent actions stop at preview or approval request states and do not execute direct Jira changes without the required approval boundary.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: When required inputs are missing, ambiguous, or unresolved, the product asks for clarification instead of guessing or silently failing.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Review scenarios across search, issue lookup, assignee resolution, and change-request flows show explicit clarification prompts for missing or ambiguous project, issue, user, sprint, status, or relationship inputs.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Approval-required Jira change requests provide enough preview detail and business reason context for a change approver to review the intended action before execution.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM review of workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue link request examples shows the target change, affected Jira items, and stated reason are visible at approval time.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: The showcase produces reviewable audit evidence for governed reads, denials, previews, approvals, and approved change execution paths.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Audit review can inspect traceable records for representative read, denial, preview, approval-stop, and approved-action scenarios without reconstructing the flow from raw backend logs alone.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Release-note preparation produces a bounded draft from Jira issue and release context without publishing externally or mutating Jira.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Scenario review shows release-note preparation returns a draft artifact based on bounded Jira inputs and does not trigger Jira updates or external dispatch.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can search backlog context, inspect issue context, prepare incident bugs, prepare subtasks, and prepare customer escalation comments within governed input rules and clarification flows.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Operational user who inspects bounded Jira issue and backlog context and prepares issue or comment drafts without direct raw Jira tool access.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Can initiate preview-oriented and approval-gated requests but should stop at explicit approval boundaries for governed mutations.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see bounded Jira project, issue, comment, changelog, and backlog context exposed through governed capabilities, plus previews and audit-relevant request context. Should not see raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or unbounded JQL/tool access.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, and issue links through governed preview and request flows with stated business reasons.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Planning-oriented user who prepares and requests governed changes to issue workflow, sprint placement, assignee, and issue relationships.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Can assemble and submit approval-required change requests, but execution authority is separate unless you confirm this actor is also an approver.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see actor-visible workflow, sprint, assignee-resolution, and issue-link preview context needed to understand proposed changes, but not raw backend operations or unrestricted Jira administration surfaces.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can prepare release notes drafts from governed Jira release and issue queries and tailor drafts for internal, customer, or executive audiences where allowed.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Release-focused user who prepares bounded release notes from Jira issue context without publishing or mutating Jira.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Can prepare release-note drafts directly; approval or publication authority is not specified in the brief and should be clarified if needed.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see bounded release and issue context sufficient to draft release notes for an intended audience, without raw export or unrestricted issue extraction.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue-link requests and decide whether they should proceed under governance.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Reviewing actor who evaluates governed Jira change requests before any approval-required mutation proceeds.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Acts as the explicit approval boundary for approval-required Jira changes, but exact authority by action type is not specified in the source and should be confirmed.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect the proposed change, target issue or sprint context, stated reason, and supporting audit evidence needed to approve or deny governed Jira mutations.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review evidence of bounded reads, prepared write-adjacent actions, approval stops, and explicit denials of raw Jira/JQL/tool access.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Oversight actor who reviews audit evidence for governed Jira access, prepared changes, approvals, and denials.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Typically not the operational approver unless your process combines audit and approval responsibilities; this should be clarified.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect audit evidence showing what governed capability was used, what was requested or denied, and where approval boundaries were enforced. Should not gain raw Jira tool access through the audit role.

sections.label: Authority & Approval
sections.description: How restrictions, denials, approvals, and clarification posture become formal service behavior.

linked_product_design_items.label: Approval Posture Summary
linked_product_design_items.detail: Read-only context retrieval can proceed within bounded scope, while write-adjacent actions either stop at preview or require explicit approval before any Jira mutation is executed. Higher-impact changes are approval-gated and auditable.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Triage Operator approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can initiate preview-oriented and approval-gated requests but should stop at explicit approval boundaries for governed mutations.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Delivery Coordinator approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can assemble and submit approval-required change requests, but execution authority is separate unless you confirm this actor is also an approver.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Release Preparer approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can prepare release-note drafts directly; approval or publication authority is not specified in the brief and should be clarified if needed.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Change Approver approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Acts as the explicit approval boundary for approval-required Jira changes, but exact authority by action type is not specified in the source and should be confirmed.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Audit Reviewer approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Typically not the operational approver unless your process combines audit and approval responsibilities; this should be clarified.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Triage Operator · Bounded Jira Context
linked_product_design_items.detail: Bounded · Bounded Result · Allow bounded Jira backlog and issue context needed for triage without exposing raw JQL, raw tool access, or unbounded export.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Triage Operator · Issue and Comment Preparation
linked_product_design_items.detail: Bounded · Bounded Result · Allow preparation previews for incident bugs, subtasks, and customer escalation comments without creating issues or posting comments directly.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Delivery Coordinator · Governed Change Requests
linked_product_design_items.detail: Approval Required · Approval Stop · Allow workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue-link requests to be prepared with preview and audit context, then stop for explicit approval before execution.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Release Preparer · Release Note Preparation
linked_product_design_items.detail: Bounded · Bounded Result · Allow preparation of release-note drafts from bounded Jira issue and release context without publishing or mutating Jira.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Change Approver · Governed Change Requests
linked_product_design_items.detail: Approval Required · Approval Stop · Receive governed change-request packages with preview and rationale, and act as the explicit approval boundary before any Jira mutation proceeds.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Audit Reviewer · Governance and Audit Controls
linked_product_design_items.detail: Allowed · Direct Result · Allow access to governed audit evidence and review artifacts for Jira fronting decisions, approvals, and denials.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Triage Operator · Governance and Audit Controls
linked_product_design_items.detail: Denied · Deny Request · Deny requests for raw Jira REST access, MCP access, raw JQL execution, or direct tool use outside the governed capability surface.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Delivery Coordinator · Governance and Audit Controls
linked_product_design_items.detail: Denied · Deny Request · Deny direct raw Jira, MCP, and raw JQL access even when coordinating change requests; use governed request surfaces only.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Release Preparer · Governance and Audit Controls
linked_product_design_items.detail: Denied · Deny Request · Deny raw Jira/JQL/tool access for release-note work; release-note preparation must use bounded governed capabilities only.

linked_product_design_items.label: Jira Triage Operator · Bounded Jira Context
linked_product_design_items.detail: Restricted · Clarification Required · When required Jira identifiers or scope inputs are missing or ambiguous, stop and ask for clarification instead of guessing project, issue, status, or query scope.

linked_product_design_items.label: What caller-verification posture should the business require?
linked_product_design_items.detail: signed

linked_product_design_items.label: Should agents be able to discover what they are allowed to do before acting?
linked_product_design_items.detail: Yes

linked_product_design_items.label: Should the system distinguish between restricted and denied actions?
linked_product_design_items.detail: Yes

linked_product_design_items.label: Will this system use delegation tokens to carry scoped authority?
linked_product_design_items.detail: Yes

linked_product_design_items.label: Should authority be scoped to limit what agents can do?
linked_product_design_items.detail: Yes

linked_product_design_items.label: Should delegated authority be bound to a specific purpose?
linked_product_design_items.detail: Yes

linked_product_design_items.label: Deny Direct Raw Jira, Raw JQL, And Raw Tool Access In Favor Of Governed Business Capabilities.

linked_product_design_items.label: Preserve Audit Evidence For Governed Reads, Previews, Requests, And Approvals.
linked_product_design_items.detail: Business goal the implementation should help achieve.

linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Behavior Summary
linked_product_design_items.detail: The product exposes governed Jira capabilities instead of raw Jira operations. Reads return bounded actor-visible context, write-adjacent actions produce previews or requests rather than direct execution, missing required inputs trigger clarification, and unsafe raw access requests are explicitly denied.

runtime_policy_bindings.business_rule: Allow bounded Jira backlog and issue context needed for triage without exposing raw JQL, raw tool access, or unbounded export.

Allow bounded Jira backlog and issue context needed for triage without exposing raw JQL, raw tool access, or unbounded export. Matches bounded context search and issue context inspection in the brief.

runtime_policy_bindings.business_rule: Allow preparation previews for incident bugs, subtasks, and customer escalation comments without creating issues or posting comments directly.
runtime_policy_bindings.enforcement_notes: jira_triage_operator bounded access to Issue and Comment Preparation should return bounded_result. Allow preparation previews for incident bugs, subtasks, and customer escalation comments without creating issues or posting comments directly. Preparation remains preview-only and non-mutating from the actor perspective.

runtime_policy_bindings.business_rule: Allow workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue-link requests to be prepared with preview and audit context, then stop for explicit approval before execution.
runtime_policy_bindings.enforcement_notes: jira_delivery_coordinator approval_required access to Governed Change Requests should return approval_stop. Allow workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue-link requests to be prepared with preview and audit context, then stop for explicit approval before execution. Covers approval-required write-adjacent change requests.

runtime_policy_bindings.business_rule: Allow preparation of release-note drafts from bounded Jira issue and release context without publishing or mutating Jira.
runtime_policy_bindings.enforcement_notes: jira_release_preparer bounded access to Release Note Preparation should return bounded_result. Allow preparation of release-note drafts from bounded Jira issue and release context without publishing or mutating Jira. Read-only draft generation only.

service_topology_bindings.implementation_notes:
Accepted assistant guidance:
- Formalize the capability surface each service must expose: Define the stable capability contracts each service is expected to own, including which actions are read-only, which require approval, and which must stop for clarification.
  Why: The generator and verifier need stable capability ownership, not only narrative service descriptions.
- Describe the required coordination edges between services: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.
  Why: Coordination edges are easier to validate when they are declared before implementation.
- Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.
  Why: Approval semantics should be explicit in service design rather than buried in one implementation path.
- Preserve explanation and traceability across service boundaries: Define which service boundaries must preserve explanation, lineage, or durable evidence so later verification can reconstruct why a result or stop condition occurred.
  Why: Cross-service traceability should be deliberate, not inferred after implementation exists.
